CONSENT JUDGMENT ( Judge Joan N. Ericksen ) AND ORDER FOR JUDGMENT. The carbon composite gun barrels (Accused Product) do not infringe the '912 patent. All causes of action, claims and counterclaims in this civil action arising out of defts manufacture and sale of the Accused Product are dismissed with prejudice. Any and all claims and or defenses as they relate to products materially different than the Accused Product, including, without limitation, any claims or defenses relating to carbon composite gun barrels are expressly preserved. 3pgs (c: attys.) CONSENT JUDGMENT: by Judge Ann D. Montgomery re: 3M Innovative properties owns the '640 patent, 3M Co is the exclusive licensee and plaintffs have standing to bring this action. GlSynthesis waives the right to contest the validity and/or enforceability of the '640 patent. GLSynthesis are permanently enjoined from infringing the '640 patent. This injunction shall not extend beyond the date of the expiration of the '640 patent. Within 10 days GLSynthesis shall serve upon the counsel for plaintiffs a written declaration confirming the GLSynthesis has complied with all terms of this judgment. If GLSynthesis is found by the court to be in contempt of or to have violated this judgment 3M will suffer irreparable harm and shall be entitled in addition to any damages caused by GLSynthesis contempt or violation of this judgment to recover its attorneys' fee, costs and other expenses incurred in eforcing this judgment. Plaintiffs' request that Anderson be added or substituted as an inventor on the '430 patent is hereby denied.
